text.title: Solo Go/No-Go Criteria — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every go/no-go decision must enforce. These are HARD rules — sunk-cost attachment cannot override them.

rule.statement: PRE-COMMITMENT: The thresholds MUST be written and dated BEFORE the first interview or validation experiment; thresholds adjusted after data collection are voided and the decision defaults to no-go.

rationale.source: Pre-registration practice in empirical research + Kahneman planning-fallacy work

rule.rationale: Post-hoc threshold revision is the canonical way founders rationalize bad ideas; pre-commitment is the only credible defense.

rule.statement: FOUR NUMERIC THRESHOLDS: problem-frequency (% of ICP that confirms the pain ≥ once/week), willingness-to-pay (% of confirmed-pain who say a specific $ figure unprompted), channel-cost (estimated CAC less than estimated 12-month gross profit per customer), founder-fit (founder energy/expertise scored 1–5 ≥ 3). All four MUST be specified with concrete numbers, not "high"/"strong."

rationale.source: ICP validation + unit economics (Sean Ellis / David Skok)

rule.rationale: Verbal thresholds rationalize away; numeric thresholds either pass or fail.

rule.statement: FAIL-CLOSED: A go verdict requires ALL four thresholds to pass; failing ANY threshold = kill. No "3 out of 4 with a strong founder-fit makes up for it" reasoning is permitted in the verdict document.

rationale.source: Conjunctive-validation discipline (Y Combinator interview corpus / Steve Blank customer-discovery)

rule.rationale: Compensatory scoring lets the strongest signal mask the weakest; AND-gating forces the weakest to actually be addressed.

rule.statement: EVIDENCE ATTACHMENT: Each threshold pass/fail MUST be supported by a named evidence artifact (interview_id, landing_page_metric_export, ad-platform CSV); thresholds marked "pass" without an artifact default to fail.
rule.rationale: Verdicts without artifacts are remembered selectively; the named artifact is the only protection against motivated recall.

rule.statement: KILL-DAY RITUAL: A kill verdict triggers a 24-hour cool-off before the founder may pivot or revive the idea; revival requires writing a new criteria document, not patching the original.

rationale.source: Sunk-cost de-bias research (Kahneman and Tversky / behavioural economics)

rule.rationale: The first 24 hours after a kill are when founders are most likely to talk themselves out of it; the cool-off + fresh-doc rule preserves the verdict.

example.title: Anchored go/no-go decision
example.code:
idea: "Stripe-only billing reconciliation for solo SaaS" written_at: 2026-04-01 # before any validation thresholds: problem_frequency_pct: 40 # of interviewed ICP, weekly pain willingness_to_pay_pct: 25 # of confirmed-pain, name a $ figure unprompted channel_cost_lte_12m_gross_profit: true founder_fit_score: 4
verdict_date: 2026-05-15 verdict_inputs: problem_frequency_pct_observed: 47 # evidence: interviews/INT-001..014 willingness_to_pay_pct_observed: 14 # evidence: outreach/wtp-export.csv channel_cost_lte_12m_gross_profit_observed: true # evidence: ads/test-2026-05.csv founder_fit_score_observed: 4
verdict: kill rationale: "willingness_to_pay 14% fails the 25% threshold" cooloff_until: 2026-05-16T15:00Z
example.note: Pre-committed, four numeric thresholds, evidence-backed, fail-closed, 24h cool-off honored.

example.title: Un-anchored go/no-go decision
example.code: "Did 8 interviews, people seemed interested, I have a strong feeling about this. Going to build."
example.note: No thresholds, no numbers, no evidence, no fail-closed rule. The "strong feeling" is a 100% predictor of build → ship → no revenue → abandonment 6 months later.
